data.description:

data.title: SEC’s crypto task force lead criticizes ETF approval process, highlights need for patience
data.description:
The head of the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Crypto Task Force, Hester Peirce, said the decade-long process that led to the approval of spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) was mishandled, and urged crypto market participants to be patient with ongoing regulatory delays.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/secs-crypto-task-force-lead-criticizes-etf-approval-process-highlights-need-for-patience/

data.title: SEC Files for $50 Million Settlement With Ripple Over XRP Lawsuit
data.description:
The SEC and Ripple agreed that the fintech company will pay just $50 million to settle a long-running lawsuit over XRP sales.
source: https://decrypt.co/318636/sec-ripple-50-million-settlement-xrp

data.title: Trump Proposes Tax Increase for Wealthiest Americans
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump is advocating for a tax increase on the wealthiest Americans to balance other tax cuts in his economic plan. The proposal suggests a new tax bracket of 39.6% for individuals earning at least $2.5 million annually or couples earning $5 million. If approved by Congress, this would restore the top tax rate to its pre-2017 levels, reversing Trump's previous tax cuts. The current top individual tax rate stands at 37%. Trump made this proposal during a phone call with House Speaker Johnson on Wednesday, reiterating his desire to eliminate the carried interest tax break for venture capital and private equity fund managers.

data.title: Wellgistics Health integrates XRP into payment network to improve medical payment efficiency
data.description: Wellgistics Health announced that it will integrate XRP and related technologies into its healthcare provider payment network to "streamline transactions between pharmacies, medical suppliers, and prescription drug manufacturers." The company emphasized that XRP's transaction "finality time" and "transaction costs are only a fraction of a cent" are far superior to traditional financial systems such as ACH or wire transfers. The integration will "reduce cross-border friction" and "allow transactions between different companies in the supply chain to be settled in real time."

data.title: Meta is considering integrating stablecoins to reduce payment costs after selling the Diem project
data.description: Meta is considering integrating stablecoins to reduce the cost of payments compared to fiat currencies, such as paying Instagram creators. The report said that "the company does not seem to have yet to determine which stablecoin to use". In addition, Meta has "contacted crypto infrastructure companies to solve the cost of cross-regional payments". The company has also hired "former Ripple executive Ginger Baker as vice president of product". The renewed focus on stablecoins comes at a time when "US regulators have taken a friendlier attitude towards cryptocurrencies after President Trump took office". In 2019, Meta launched the stablecoin project Libra, which was later renamed Diem, but "due to regulatory suppression, the project failed to launch as scheduled and was eventually closed". Diem's ​​intellectual property has been sold to Silvergate Bank, and although the bank later declared bankruptcy, "the project continues in new chains such as Aptos in the MOVE language, but no longer involves Meta."

data.title: Trump: Talking to Fed Chairman Powell is like "playing the lute to a cow"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Trump said that everyone except the Federal Reserve is cutting interest rates. Talking to Federal Reserve Chairman Powell is like "playing the lute to a cow". Federal Reserve Chairman Powell is always "too late". Federal Reserve Chairman Powell may not like me. Even without cutting interest rates, the United States is still doing well.

data.title: Meta Explores Stablecoin Integration Amid Growing Institutional Interest
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, tech giant Meta is reportedly considering the integration of stablecoin payments into its platforms, marking a potential return to the cryptocurrency space after a three-year hiatus. The Facebook parent company has engaged in discussions with various crypto infrastructure firms, although no definitive course of action has been decided, sources familiar with the matter revealed.

One insider suggested that Meta might adopt a multi-token strategy, potentially incorporating support for widely-used stablecoins such as Tether's USDt (USDT) and Circle's USD Coin (USDC), among others. This move aligns with a broader trend among tech companies exploring stablecoin usage for payments, driven by increasing institutional interest and investment. The stablecoin market capitalization has surged beyond $230 billion, reflecting this growing demand.

In recent developments, several payment processing companies have announced investments in stablecoin firms or unveiled stablecoin integrations. On May 7, Visa disclosed its investment in stablecoin startup BVNK, although specific details of the deal remain limited. Rubail Birwadker, Visa's head of products and partnerships, noted that stablecoins are capturing a significant share of the payments market.

Stripe, another global payments platform, introduced stablecoin-based accounts for customers across more than 100 countries on the same day. These accounts enable users to store stablecoin balances, transfer tokens to other users, and convert stablecoin holdings into fiat currency for withdrawal to traditional bank accounts.

World Liberty Financial (WLFI), a crypto firm backed by U.S. President Donald Trump, launched USD1, a stablecoin pegged to the US dollar, in March. By May, USD1 had become the seventh-largest stablecoin by market capitalization, underscoring the rapid expansion of the tokenized fiat market. The Trump administration has consistently emphasized the strategic importance of stablecoins in U.S. policy, viewing them as a means to bolster US dollar dominance by leveraging demand for US government Treasurys and other securities.

Despite these advancements, comprehensive stablecoin regulations faced a setback on May 8 when Democratic Senators blocked the GENIUS Stablecoin bill, disappointing senior officials in the Trump administration. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent expressed his frustration in a May 8 X post, stating, "The Senate missed an opportunity to provide leadership today by failing to advance the GENIUS Act. This bill represents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to expand dollar dominance."

The post USD1 Stablecoin Blasts 1,540% to $2

data.title: Bill Gates announces he will donate most of his wealth to the Gates Foundation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Microsoft founder Bill Gates announced on the 8th that he would donate most of his personal wealth to the Gates Foundation and set a goal of more than $200 billion in charitable spending before the foundation ceases operations in 2045 to support people in need around the world. He said that the Gates Foundation's charitable spending is expected to exceed $200 billion from now to 2045, including the size of the foundation's existing endowment fund and his future donations. According to a statement released by the Gates Foundation, the foundation's overall strategy for the next 20 years will remain unchanged. It will work closely with partners to do its utmost to promote three core goals, namely, to help end maternal and infant deaths from preventable diseases; to ensure that the next generation is no longer threatened by deadly infectious diseases; and to help hundreds of millions of people escape poverty and embark on the road to prosperity.

data.title: India asked X to block more than 8,000 accounts in India, but X disagreed with the Indian government's request.
data.description: Social Media Platform "X": Has received an administrative order from the Government of India requiring X to block over 8,000 accounts in India. The order includes a request to block access to accounts belonging to international news organizations and prominent X users in India. Disagree with the Indian Government's request. Exploring all possible legal avenues for the Company.
